questions: Keep things measured and level-headed. Your best friend just texted saying she's moving back to your city after three years away and wants to know if there are any good apartments in your neighborhood — what do you tell her?

Keep things measured and level-headed. You just found out your short story won first place in a competition you'd almost given up on — the organizer emails asking for your bio and a headshot. What do you send back?

Keep things measured and level-headed. Your band just played its first completely sold-out show and the crowd was electric — the venue manager emails the next morning asking if you'd like to book another date. How do you reply?

Keep things measured and level-headed. Your team just got the greenlight on a project you've been pitching for over a year — your manager asks you to book a kickoff meeting for the group. What do you say?

Keep things measured and level-headed. Your kid just called to say they got into their first-choice university — they're asking if you can help them look at dorm options this weekend. How do you reply?
Keep things measured and level-headed. A trip you've been planning for two years finally got approved and booked — your travel companion asks what you want to pack first. What do you tell them?

Keep things measured and level-headed. You just ran your first 10K and finished under the goal time you've been training toward for months — a friend who was watching asks if you want to grab food afterward. What do you say?

Keep things measured and level-headed. The mentor who shaped your career just emailed out of nowhere to say they've been following your work and they're proud of you — they ask if you'd like to catch up over coffee. How do you reply?

Keep things measured and level-headed. You just found out your favorite author is doing a rare local reading next month and tickets just went on sale — a friend texts asking if you want to go together. What do you say?

Keep things measured and level-headed. Your partner surprises you by saying yes to getting a dog you've both talked about for years — they ask which shelter you want to visit first. How do you respond?
